title: Critical review of Phase 0 development spec for cross-orchestrator handoff pipeline. Read this spec file first: ~/.claude/cache/dev-spec-phase0-handoff-pipeline.md Then read the actual source files that will be modified: 1. ~/.claude/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-lane-bridge.mjs (f...
summary_text: The spec is not executable as written against the current tree. The biggest problem is that it assumes a live `codex-plugin` lane and wrapper file that do not exist. **P0** - `delegate-codex-plugin.js` is not present in the live tree, and `codex-plugin` is not a supported bridge provider. The current provider map in [fugue-lane-bridge.mjs](/Users/masayuki/.claude/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-lane-bridge.mjs#L52) only includes `codex`, `codex-spark`, `glm`, `gemini`, and `grok`, and failover code also knows nothing about `codex-plugin` in [failover.js](/Users/masayuki/.claude/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/lib/failover.js#L14). Proposed fix: either restore/add a real plugin delegate wrapper and wire provider registration/failover/tests, or remove all `codex-plugin` claims from Phase 0. - The spec places context generation in `spawnLane()`, but that function does not have the data needed. Its signature is just `(command, args, options)` in [fugue-lane-bridge.mjs](/Users/masayuki/.claude/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-lane-bridge.mjs#L328), so it has no `provider`, `agent`, `task`, `project`, or `runId`. Proposed fix: write delegation context in `runSingleAttempt()` before the spawn call, or extend `buildSpawnSpec()` to return provider-specific context metadata. - The single global `~/.claude/cache/delegation-context.md` is a race condition under matrix execution. `handleMatrix()` runs lanes concurrently via `Promise.allSettled(...)` in [fugue-lane-bridge.mjs](/Users/masayuki/.claude/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-lane-bridge.mjs#L689), so multiple lanes can overwrite each other’s handoff file. Proposed fix: use a per-attempt file such as `~/.claude/cache/delegation-context/<runId>.md` and pass that exact path to the delegate. - The spec’s “atomic write” snippet is still unsafe under concurrency because it uses a fixed temp file suffix (`.tmp`). Two writers can collide on the temp path before the rename. The bridge already has a safer pattern in `writeJsonAtomic()` in [fugue-lane-bridge.mjs](/Users/masayuki/.claude/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-lane-bridge.mjs#L140). Proposed fix: reuse that unique-temp-path pattern, but also stop sharing one final filename across lanes. - ACPX mode bypasses `delegate.js` entirely. `buildSpawnSpec()` early-returns an ACPX command for providers listed in `FUGUE_ACPX_PROVIDERS` in [fugue-lane-bridge.mjs](/Users/masayuki/.claude/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-lane-bridge.mjs#L285). Proposed fix: either scope this feature to non-ACPX paths or add equivalent context injection for ACPX transport. **P1** - The manual smoke command in the spec is invalid for the current bridge CLI. The bridge accepts `--lane provider:agent --task ...` and does not parse `-a`, `-t`, `--run-id`, or `--dry-run`, as shown in [fugue-lane-bridge.mjs](/Users/masayuki/.claude/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-lane-bridge.mjs#L69) and [fugue-lane-bridge.mjs](/Users/masayuki/.claude/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-lane-bridge.mjs#L609). Proposed fix: rewrite the smoke test to use the real CLI shape, or add an actual dry-run mode first. - The security claim is too weak. Writing `${task.slice(0, 800)}` to disk is not a “safe summary”; it can contain secrets, customer data, URLs, or raw code pasted into the task. Proposed fix: run the task through the existing `redactSecrets` path already used for persisted lane outputs in [fugue-lane-bridge.mjs](/Users/masayuki/.claude/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-lane-bridge.mjs#L261), and prefer a minimal structured summary over raw task text. - Existing direct `delegate.js` callers bypass the bridge, so the spec does not solve end-to-end handoff for all Codex flows. There are direct call sites in `detect-delegator-task.sh`, `debate-evidence.js`, `evaluate.js`, `dispatcher.mjs`, `consensus-vote.js`, and `parallel-codex.js` from the repo-wide search. Pr...
